records.ety: [F. complot, prob. for comploit, fr.L. complicitum, prop. p. p. of complicare, but equiv. to complicatio complication, entangling. See Complicate, and cf. Plot.]

defs: A plotting together; a confederacy in some evil design; a conspiracy.
quotes: I know their complot is to have my life.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Complotted; p. pr. & vb. n. Complotting.]

defs: To plot or plan together; to conspire; to join in a secret design.
quotes: We find them complotting together, and contriving a new scene of miseries to the Trojans.
